wiki.content: “I am not a Lyricist” is the sixth track on Baby Keem’s sophomore album, Ca$ino. The song was first teased alongside, “$ex Appeal”, on the YouTube video, Booman III Ca$ino Documentary, posted on February 19, 2026. The title of the track suggests that Baby Keem does not see himself as a “Lyricist” (which he technically is by definition, but he’s referring to rap lyricists who have complexities in their lyrics) and that the song will reinforce this idea, however, in this song Baby Keem uses somewhat of a blend of a “Sing About Me, I’m Dying of Thirst” Kendrick and André 3000 cadence throughout the song initially contradicting the title since Kendrick and André 3000 are critically acclaimed to be two of the most lyrically heavy artists ever. However, in dissecting the lyrics we see that Keem doesn’t use any complex rhyme patterns or metaphors and quintiple entendres (“I’m not here to play off words”) that we expect from a typical “Lyricist” like Kendrick or André 3000, he instead uses simple patterns, annunciates his lyrics very clearly and raps about the usual stuff he does throughout the album, nothing fancy just the basics of conventional rap so this would reinforce his sentiments about not being a “Lyricist”. So, the execution initially contradicts the title and makes it seem like situational irony but the content and purpose of the song eventually shows that indeed Baby Keem is in fact not a lyricist because he chooses not to be. The irony though, if any, is that this is probably his most lyrical song to date. Read more on Last.fm.
